Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE1: Search for emails to and from elena.rostova@moveops.com (via search_emails) to identify recent threads, open items, and unanswered messages. Key discoveries: Elena sent Marcus Thorne a directive requesting a formal vendor strategy memo by Wednesday; Chloe Vance sent Elena asking about the Terraform coordinator hire status with no reply from Elena; David Chen sent Elena requesting authority for a temporary staffing redistribution with no reply from Elena.
OE2: Search for emails related to client escalations and operational issues (via search_emails) using queries such as "NorthWind", "StormCloud", "Mosaic", "damage", "escalation", "urgent", "vendor", "DOT", "ExpenseBot" to find the NorthWind formal retention escalation from Pam Kowalski to David Chen demanding a corrective plan (citing Raj Patel security breach and Emilia Cruz piano damage, mentioning PathFinder Logistics as a competitor), the StormCloud CFO escalation from Liam Park to Mina with 4 unresolved issues (Jae-won Kim housing not finalized, $500 goodwill credit not yet applied to the invoice, Simone Richter reimbursement stuck, Ridgeline Robotics referral never followed up), the Mosaic Robotics $90K prototype damage incident, the DOT compliance inquiry (Case No. PHMSA-2026-04-1187) with June 5 deadline, the Heartland and Swift vendor rate increase notices, the Terraform Digital reply-all pricing leak, the BrightLoop Analytics 6-relocation expansion request from Tessa Moreno, the ExpenseBot false approvals and rejections emails, and the coordinator capacity crisis flagged by Chloe and David.
OE3: Search Slack messages (via conversations_search_messages) for recent internal discussions across channels including topics like "vendor", "Heartland", "Swift", "capacity", "Mosaic", "NorthWind", "StormCloud", "BrightLoop", "Terraform", "ExpenseBot", "Wanderlust", and "DOT" to find team discussions about vendor rate increases (Heartland 20% Q3 increase, Swift 15% immediate increase plus $500 DOT surcharge), coordinator capacity constraints, client escalations, the Wanderlust legacy data migration status and blockers, and engineering issues including the ExpenseBot remediation.
OE4: Search CRM for company records of active and at-risk client accounts (via crm_search_companies) for Mosaic Robotics, BrightLoop Analytics, Vantage Distributed (Terraform Digital), NorthWind Technologies, Vectral Systems, StormCloud Analytics, and Canopy Health to find account statuses, relationship context, and risk flags. Key discoveries: Mosaic Robotics is the newest client (approximately 2 months), type Client, account at risk due to $90K prototype damage incident; BrightLoop Analytics is a newer client relocating data science staff to Boston HQ; Vantage Distributed (Terraform Digital) is a $1.85M enterprise deal with a reply-all pricing leak incident; Vectral Systems has a Q3 renewal at risk with board decision around April 23; NorthWind Technologies is in account recovery mode after Q1 CSAT of 5/10 with a formal escalation; Canopy Health has its first C-suite relocation (Dr. Anand Mehta, CMO, $12,170 deal).
OE5: List CRM engagements for key client accounts (via crm_list_engagements, filtered by company_ids) to find detailed engagement notes documenting the Mosaic damage incident timeline and $40K insurance gap, the NorthWind Denver expansion recovery context, the Vectral renewal negotiation log, the Terraform reply-all incident log, the BrightLoop expansion notes, and the Canopy Health executive relocation note.
OE6: Search Linear for open and stalled issues across Engineering and Operations teams (via linear_list_issues, filtered by team and/or state) to find engineering tickets related to ExpenseBot bugs (e.g., ENG-216 full audit of pilot expenses), the Wanderlust legacy data migration (ENG-227, overdue, 47 duplicate vendors, slipping timeline), DOT compliance tasks (missing Keiko Tanaka placarding certificate), the dormant invoice email template safety check ticket (ENG-187, assigned to Lena Björkström but in backlog since April 6, whose non-implementation enabled the Sunbelt/Palmetto invoice swap documented in OPS-417), the NorthWind retention response plan, the StormCloud incident reconstruction, the BrightLoop account audit, and any overdue or unassigned operations issues.
OE7: Search Airtable for in-progress relocations (via airtable_search_records on the Relocations table, field "Status", value "In Progress") to find all active relocation records and identify any flagged or stalled moves. Key discoveries: multiple in-progress relocations across Canopy Health, Vectral Systems, GreenStack Energy, PivotPoint Labs, BrightLoop Analytics, Mosaic Robotics, and NorthWind Technologies; Jae-won Kim (StormCloud) has Status "In Progress" but a note indicating the lease is still unsigned because UrbanNest flagged a landlord holdout on guarantor paperwork, suggesting the move has stalled; Simone Richter (BrightLoop) has an urgent rush relocation with lease already ended April 6.
OE8: Search Airtable for stipend transaction records with pending or flagged statuses (via airtable_search_records on the Stipend Transactions table, field "Approval Status", value "Pending" or "Pending Approval") to find outstanding stipend claims. Key discoveries: Priya Venkatesh (GreenStack) $379 noise-canceling headphones with missing receipt; Kevin Tran (Vectral) $129 monitor arm mount; Ines Kovac (PivotPoint) $144 Figma Professional license; Andre Washington (Mosaic) $89 ergonomic keyboard flagged with duplicate receipt hash identical to Kevin Tran's submission; Jordan Ekwueme (BrightLoop) $45 monitor stand flagged with matching PDF template metadata from the same vendor (TechComfort Supply).
OE9: Look up Elena Rostova's calendar for the rest of the week through Friday (via calendar_get_calendar_events_from_to with start_datetime and end_datetime covering today through Friday) to identify existing meetings, conflicts, back-to-backs, and available time slots for scheduling with Marcus and Chloe. Also check whether a "leadership review" on Friday is already on the calendar.
OE10: Look up contact details for Marcus Thorne and Chloe Vance (via contacts_search_contacts with queries "Marcus Thorne" and "Chloe Vance") to find their email addresses (marcus.thorne@moveops.com, chloe.vance@moveops.com) for sending follow-up emails and calendar invites.
OE11: Send follow-up emails on Elena's behalf (via send_email) from priya.chakrabarti@moveops.com (or elena.rostova@moveops.com) to team members with outstanding items. Specific nudges should include: Marcus Thorne regarding the vendor strategy memo status (due Wednesday), referencing Elena's directive and requesting a progress update; Chloe Vance regarding the Terraform coordinator hire that Elena authorized but that was never posted, asking for a status update on why the posting has not moved forward; David Chen responding to his unanswered request for temporary staffing authorization, providing a response or status update on Elena's behalf. Emails should reference the specific open thread and request a status update or next step.
OE12: Create a calendar event for Elena and Marcus to discuss the budget situation before Friday (via calendar_add_calendar_event) with title related to budget/vendor strategy discussion, attendees including elena.rostova@moveops.com and marcus.thorne@moveops.com, scheduled at an available time slot that does not conflict with existing calendar events.
OE13: Create a calendar event for Elena and Chloe to discuss the operations backlog before Friday (via calendar_add_calendar_event) with title related to operations/capacity discussion, attendees including elena.rostova@moveops.com and chloe.vance@moveops.com, scheduled at a separate available time slot that does not conflict with existing events or the Marcus meeting.
OE14: Search Airtable for client account records (via airtable_search_records on the Client Accounts table, or airtable_list_records on table "Client Accounts") to find client relationship statuses, contract types, and account notes. Key discoveries: Mosaic Robotics has Onboarding status and is flagged as critical for retention; Vectral Systems notes document the Q3 contract renegotiation with a 15% discount demand, a 10% counter-offer, and board decision pending around April 23; NorthWind Technologies notes reference timeline and quality issues with the account; StormCloud Analytics has 3 relocations on a premium package.
OE15: Search QuickBooks for financial records relevant to vendor disputes, liability exposure, and budget context (via quickbooks_search_bills and quickbooks_search_invoices) to find the Mosaic damage pending liability accrual of $90,000 (bill_mosaic_damage_accrual_001), the DOT/PHMSA contingent penalty accrual of $50,000 (bill_dot_penalty_accrual_q2), the Heartland Movers Q1 bill under dispute with a $12,800 payment hold, and vendor balance data for Heartland ($12,800) and Swift ($11,010) relevant to the dual rate increase situation.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
